ayahs.textArabic: طه
ayahs.textTransliteration: Taha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Ta, Ha.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ṭâ-Hâ.

ayahs.textArabic: مَآ أَنزَلۡنَا عَلَيۡكَ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِتَشۡقَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma anzalna AAalayka alqurana litashqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have not sent down to you the Qur'an that you be distressed
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have not revealed the Quran to you ˹O Prophet˺ to cause you distress,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا تَذۡكِرَةٗ لِّمَن يَخۡشَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa tathkiratan liman yakhsha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But only as a reminder for those who fear [Allah] -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but as a reminder to those in awe ˹of Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: تَنزِيلٗا مِّمَّنۡ خَلَقَ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ ٱلۡعُلَى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tanzeelan mimman khalaqa alarda waalssamawati alAAula
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A revelation from He who created the earth and highest heavens,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It is˺ a revelation from the One Who created the earth and the high heavens—

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alrrahmanu AAala alAAarshi istawa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Most Merciful [who is] above the Throne established.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Most Compassionate, ˹Who is˺ established on the Throne.

ayahs.textArabic: لَهُۥ مَا فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا وَمَا تَحۡتَ ٱلثَّرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lahu ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi wama baynahuma wama tahta alththara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To Him belongs what is in the heavens and what is on the earth and what is between them and what is under the soil.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth and whatever is in between and whatever is underground.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن تَجۡهَرۡ بِٱلۡقَوۡلِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ وَأَخۡفَى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain tajhar bialqawli fainnahu yaAAlamu alssirra waakhfa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if you speak aloud - then indeed, He knows the secret and what is [even] more hidden.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whether you speak openly ˹or not˺, He certainly knows what is secret and what is even more hidden.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۖ لَهُ ٱلۡأَسۡمَآءُ ٱلۡحُسۡنَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwa lahu alasmao alhusna
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah - there is no deity except Him. To Him belong the best names.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah—there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. He has the Most Beautiful Names.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahal ataka hadeethu moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has the story of Moses reached you? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the story of Moses reached you ˹O Prophet˺?

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ رَءَا نَارٗا فَقَالَ لِأَهۡلِهِ ٱمۡكُثُوٓاْ إِنِّيٓ ءَانَسۡتُ نَارٗا لَّعَلِّيٓ ءَاتِيكُم مِّنۡهَا بِقَبَسٍ أَوۡ أَجِدُ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ هُدٗى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith raa naran faqala liahlihi omkuthoo innee anastu naran laAAallee ateekum minha biqabasin aw ajidu AAala alnnari hudan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he saw a fire and said to his family, "Stay here; indeed, I have perceived a fire; perhaps I can bring you a torch or find at the fire some guidance."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When he saw a fire, he said to his family, “Wait here, ˹for˺ I have spotted a fire. Perhaps I can bring you a torch from it, or find some guidance at the fire.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِيَ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma ataha noodiya ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when he came to it, he was called, "O Moses,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But when he approached it, he was called, “O Moses!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنِّيٓ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكَ فَٱخۡلَعۡ نَعۡلَيۡكَ إِنَّكَ بِٱلۡوَادِ ٱلۡمُقَدَّسِ طُوٗى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innee ana rabbuka faikhlaAA naAAlayka innaka bialwadi almuqaddasi tuwan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am your Lord, so remove your sandals. Indeed, you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is truly I. I am your Lord! So take off your sandals, for you are in the sacred valley of Ṭuwa.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَا ٱخۡتَرۡتُكَ فَٱسۡتَمِعۡ لِمَا يُوحَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waana ikhtartuka faistamiAA lima yooha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed [to you].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I have chosen you, so listen to what is revealed:

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّنِيٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعۡبُدۡنِي وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكۡرِيٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innanee ana Allahu la ilaha illa ana faoAAbudnee waaqimi alssalata lithikree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, I am Allah. There is no deity except Me, so worship Me and establish prayer for My remembrance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ‘It is truly I. I am Allah! There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Me. So worship Me ˹alone˺, and establish prayer for My remembrance.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ ءَاتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخۡفِيهَا لِتُجۡزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفۡسِۭ بِمَا تَسۡعَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna alssaAAata atiyatun akadu okhfeeha litujza kullu nafsin bima tasAAa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the Hour is coming - I almost conceal it - so that every soul may be recompensed according to that for which it strives.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Hour is sure to come. My Will is to keep it hidden, so that every soul may be rewarded according to their efforts.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنۡهَا مَن لَّا يُؤۡمِنُ بِهَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ فَتَرۡدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fala yasuddannaka AAanha man la yuminu biha waittabaAAa hawahu fatarda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So do not let one avert you from it who does not believe in it and follows his desire, for you [then] would perish.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So do not let those who disbelieve in it and follow their desires distract you from it, or you will be doomed.’”

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا تِلۡكَ بِيَمِينِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama tilka biyameenika ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what is that in your right hand, O Moses?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah added,˺ “And what is that in your right hand, O Moses?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ هِيَ عَصَايَ أَتَوَكَّؤُاْ عَلَيۡهَا وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِي وَلِيَ فِيهَا مَـَٔارِبُ أُخۡرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala hiya AAasaya atawakkao AAalayha waahushshu biha AAala ghanamee waliya feeha maaribu okhra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "It is my staff; I lean upon it, and I bring down leaves for my sheep and I have therein other uses."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “It is my staff! I lean on it, and with it I beat down ˹branches˺ for my sheep, and have other uses for it.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَلۡقِهَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala alqiha ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "Throw it down, O Moses."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah said, “Throw it down, O Moses!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَلۡقَىٰهَا فَإِذَا هِيَ حَيَّةٞ تَسۡعَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faalqaha faitha hiya hayyatun tasAAa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he threw it down, and thereupon it was a snake, moving swiftly.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So he did, then—behold!—it became a serpent, slithering.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ خُذۡهَا وَلَا تَخَفۡۖ سَنُعِيدُهَا سِيرَتَهَا ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala khuthha wala takhaf sanuAAeeduha seerataha aloola
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "Seize it and fear not; We will return it to its former condition.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah said, “Take it, and have no fear. We will return it to its former state.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱضۡمُمۡ يَدَكَ إِلَىٰ جَنَاحِكَ تَخۡرُجۡ بَيۡضَآءَ مِنۡ غَيۡرِ سُوٓءٍ ءَايَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waodmum yadaka ila janahika takhruj baydaa min ghayri sooin ayatan okhra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And draw in your hand to your side; it will come out white without disease - another sign,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And put your hand under your armpit, it will come out ˹shining˺ white, unblemished, as another sign,

ayahs.textArabic: لِنُرِيَكَ مِنۡ ءَايَٰتِنَا ٱلۡكُبۡرَى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Linuriyaka min ayatina alkubra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That We may show you [some] of Our greater signs.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that We may show you some of Our greatest signs.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱذۡهَبۡ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ithhab ila firAAawna innahu tagha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Go to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ˹all bounds˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱشۡرَحۡ لِي صَدۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi ishrah lee sadree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "My Lord, expand for me my breast [with assurance]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses prayed, “My Lord! Uplift my heart for me,

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَسِّرۡ لِيٓ أَمۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayassir lee amree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And ease for me my task
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and make my task easy,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱحۡلُلۡ عُقۡدَةٗ مِّن لِّسَانِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waohlul AAuqdatan min lisanee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And untie the knot from my tongue
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and remove the impediment from my tongue

ayahs.textArabic: يَفۡقَهُواْ قَوۡلِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yafqahoo qawlee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That they may understand my speech.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so people may understand my speech,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱجۡعَل لِّي وَزِيرٗا مِّنۡ أَهۡلِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaijAAal lee wazeeran min ahlee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And appoint for me a minister from my family -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and grant me a helper from my family,

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰرُونَ أَخِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Haroona akhee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Aaron, my brother.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Aaron, my brother.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱشۡدُدۡ بِهِۦٓ أَزۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Oshdud bihi azree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Increase through him my strength
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Strengthen me through him,

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَشۡرِكۡهُ فِيٓ أَمۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waashrikhu fee amree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And let him share my task
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and let him share my task,

ayahs.textArabic: كَيۡ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kay nusabbihaka katheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That we may exalt You much
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that we may glorify You much

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَذۡكُرَكَ كَثِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanathkuraka katheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And remember You much.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and remember You much,

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innaka kunta bina baseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, You are of us ever Seeing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for truly You have ˹always˺ been overseeing us.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ قَدۡ أُوتِيتَ سُؤۡلَكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala qad ooteeta sulaka ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "You have been granted your request, O Moses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah responded, “All that you requested has been granted, O Moses!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ مَنَنَّا عَلَيۡكَ مَرَّةً أُخۡرَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad mananna AAalayka marratan okhra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had already conferred favor upon you another time,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And surely We had shown You favour before,

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith awhayna ila ommika ma yooha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When We inspired to your mother what We inspired,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when We inspired your mother with this:

ayahs.textArabic: أَنِ ٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ فَلۡيُلۡقِهِ ٱلۡيَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ يَأۡخُذۡهُ عَدُوّٞ لِّي وَعَدُوّٞ لَّهُۥۚ وَأَلۡقَيۡتُ عَلَيۡكَ مَحَبَّةٗ مِّنِّي وَلِتُصۡنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَيۡنِيٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ani iqthifeehi fee alttabooti faiqthifeehi fee alyammi falyulqihi alyammu bialssahili yakhuthhu AAaduwwun lee waAAaduwwun lahu waalqaytu AAalayka mahabbatan minnee walitusnaAAa AAala AAaynee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], 'Cast him into the chest and cast it into the river, and the river will throw it onto the bank; there will take him an enemy to Me and an enemy to him.' And I bestowed upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ‘Put him into a chest, then put it into the river. The river will wash it ashore, and he will be taken by ˹Pharaoh,˺ an enemy of Mine and his.’ And I blessed you with lovability from Me ˹O Moses˺ so that you would be brought up under My ˹watchful˺ Eye.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ تَمۡشِيٓ أُخۡتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَن يَكۡفُلُهُۥۖ فَرَجَعۡنَٰكَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ كَيۡ تَقَرَّ عَيۡنُهَا وَلَا تَحۡزَنَۚ وَقَتَلۡتَ نَفۡسٗا فَنَجَّيۡنَٰكَ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّٰكَ فُتُونٗاۚ فَلَبِثۡتَ سِنِينَ فِيٓ أَهۡلِ مَدۡيَنَ ثُمَّ جِئۡتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرٖ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith tamshee okhtuka fataqoolu hal adullukum AAala man yakfuluhu farajaAAnaka ila ommika kay taqarra AAaynuha wala tahzana waqatalta nafsan fanajjaynaka mina alghammi wafatannaka futoonan falabithta sineena fee ahli madyana thumma jita AAala qadarin ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And We favored you] when your sister went and said, 'Shall I direct you to someone who will be responsible for him?' So We restored you to your mother that she might be content and not grieve. And you killed someone, but We saved you from retaliation and tried you with a [severe] trial. And you remained [some] years among the people of Madyan. Then you came [here] at the decreed time, O Moses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when your sister came along and proposed, ‘Shall I direct you to someone who will nurse him?’ So We reunited you with your mother so that her heart would be put at ease, and she would not grieve. ˹Later˺ you killed a man ˹by mistake˺, but We saved you from sorrow, as well as other tests We put you through. Then you stayed for a number of years among the people of Midian. Then you came here as pre-destined, O Moses!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱصۡطَنَعۡتُكَ لِنَفۡسِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaistanaAAtuka linafsee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I produced you for Myself.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And I have selected you for My service.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱذۡهَبۡ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِـَٔايَٰتِي وَلَا تَنِيَا فِي ذِكۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ithhab anta waakhooka biayatee wala taniya fee thikree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Go, you and your brother, with My signs and do not slacken in My remembrance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Go forth, you and your brother, with My signs and never falter in remembering Me.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ithhaba ila firAAawna innahu tagha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has truly transgressed ˹all bounds˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوۡلٗا لَّيِّنٗا لَّعَلَّهُۥ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوۡ يَخۡشَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqoola lahu qawlan layyinan laAAallahu yatathakkaru aw yakhsha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear [Allah]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Speak to him gently, so perhaps he may be mindful ˹of Me˺ or fearful ˹of My punishment˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَا رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن يَفۡرُطَ عَلَيۡنَآ أَوۡ أَن يَطۡغَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbana innana nakhafu an yafruta AAalayna aw an yatgha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Our Lord, indeed we are afraid that he will hasten [punishment] against us or that he will transgress."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They both pleaded, “Our Lord! We fear that he may be quick to harm us or act tyrannically.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَآۖ إِنَّنِي مَعَكُمَآ أَسۡمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala la takhafa innanee maAAakuma asmaAAu waara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "Fear not. Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah reassured ˹them˺, “Have no fear! I am with you, hearing and seeing.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأۡتِيَاهُ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعَنَا بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبۡهُمۡۖ قَدۡ جِئۡنَٰكَ بِـَٔايَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّكَۖ وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatiyahu faqoola inna rasoola rabbika faarsil maAAana banee israeela wala tuAAaththibhum qad jinaka biayatin min rabbika waalssalamu AAala mani ittabaAAa alhuda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So go to him and say, 'Indeed, we are messengers of your Lord, so send with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace will be upon he who follows the guidance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So go to him and say, ‘Indeed we are both messengers from your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not oppress them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And salvation will be for whoever follows the ˹right˺ guidance.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا قَدۡ أُوحِيَ إِلَيۡنَآ أَنَّ ٱلۡعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna qad oohiya ilayna anna alAAathaba AAala man kaththaba watawalla
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it has been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies and turns away.' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It has indeed been revealed to us that the punishment will be upon whoever denies ˹the truth˺ and turns away.’”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala faman rabbukuma ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "So who is the Lord of you two, O Moses?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh asked, “Who then is the Lord of you two, O Moses?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِيٓ أَعۡطَىٰ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ خَلۡقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbuna allathee aAAta kulla shayin khalqahu thumma hada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Our Lord is He who gave each thing its form and then guided [it]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He answered, “Our Lord is the One Who has given everything its ˹distinctive˺ form, then guided ˹it˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلۡقُرُونِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fama balu alqurooni aloola
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "Then what is the case of the former generations?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh asked, “And what about previous peoples?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ عِلۡمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّي فِي كِتَٰبٖۖ لَّا يَضِلُّ رَبِّي وَلَا يَنسَى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala AAilmuha AAinda rabbee fee kitabin la yadillu rabbee wala yansa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "The knowledge thereof is with my Lord in a record. My Lord neither errs nor forgets."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “That knowledge is with my Lord in a Record. My Lord neither falters nor forgets ˹anything˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَهۡدٗا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمۡ فِيهَا سُبُلٗا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ فَأَخۡرَجۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّن نَّبَاتٖ شَتَّىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allathee jaAAala lakumu alarda mahdan wasalaka lakum feeha subulan waanzala mina alssamai maan faakhrajna bihi azwajan min nabatin shatta
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is He] who has made for you the earth as a bed [spread out] and inserted therein for you roadways and sent down from the sky, rain and produced thereby categories of various plants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the One˺ Who has laid out the earth for ˹all of˺ you, and set in it pathways for you, and sends down rain from the sky, causing various types of plants to grow,

ayahs.textArabic: كُلُواْ وَٱرۡعَوۡاْ أَنۡعَٰمَكُمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّأُوْلِي ٱلنُّهَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kuloo wairAAaw anAAamakum inna fee thalika laayatin liolee alnnuha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Eat [therefrom] and pasture your livestock. Indeed, in that are signs for those of intelligence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹so˺ eat and graze your cattle. Surely in this are signs for people of sound judgment.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ مِنۡهَا خَلَقۡنَٰكُمۡ وَفِيهَا نُعِيدُكُمۡ وَمِنۡهَا نُخۡرِجُكُمۡ تَارَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Minha khalaqnakum wafeeha nuAAeedukum waminha nukhrijukum taratan okhra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will extract you another time.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: From the earth We created you, and into it We will return you, and from it We will bring you back again.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَرَيۡنَٰهُ ءَايَٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad araynahu ayatina kullaha fakaththaba waaba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We certainly showed Pharaoh Our signs - all of them - but he denied and refused.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We certainly showed Pharaoh all of Our signs, but he denied them and refused ˹to believe˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَجِئۡتَنَا لِتُخۡرِجَنَا مِنۡ أَرۡضِنَا بِسِحۡرِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ajitana litukhrijana min ardina bisihrika ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Have you come to us to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He said, “Have you come to drive us out of our land with your magic, O Moses?

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَنَأۡتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحۡرٖ مِّثۡلِهِۦ فَٱجۡعَلۡ بَيۡنَنَا وَبَيۡنَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّا نُخۡلِفُهُۥ نَحۡنُ وَلَآ أَنتَ مَكَانٗا سُوٗى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falanatiyannaka bisihrin mithlihi faijAAal baynana wabaynaka mawAAidan la nukhlifuhu nahnu wala anta makanan suwan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then we will surely bring you magic like it, so make between us and you an appointment, which we will not fail to keep and neither will you, in a place assigned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We can surely meet you with similar magic. So set for us an appointment that neither of us will fail to keep, in a central place.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ مَوۡعِدُكُمۡ يَوۡمُ ٱلزِّينَةِ وَأَن يُحۡشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحٗى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala mawAAidukum yawmu alzzeenati waan yuhshara alnnasu duhan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "Your appointment is on the day of the festival when the people assemble at mid-morning."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses said, “Your appointment is on the Day of the Festival, and let the people be gathered mid-morning.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيۡدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatawalla firAAawnu fajamaAAa kaydahu thumma ata
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Pharaoh went away, put together his plan, and then came [to Moses].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh then withdrew, orchestrated his scheme, then returned.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَيۡلَكُمۡ لَا تَفۡتَرُواْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبٗا فَيُسۡحِتَكُم بِعَذَابٖۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala lahum moosa waylakum la taftaroo AAala Allahi kathiban fayushitakum biAAathabin waqad khaba mani iftara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Moses said to the magicians summoned by Pharaoh, "Woe to you! Do not invent a lie against Allah or He will exterminate you with a punishment; and he has failed who invents [such falsehood]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses warned the magicians, “Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, or He will wipe you out with a torment. Whoever fabricates ˹lies˺ is bound to fail.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَنَٰزَعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡ وَأَسَرُّواْ ٱلنَّجۡوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FatanazaAAoo amrahum baynahum waasarroo alnnajwa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they disputed over their affair among themselves and concealed their private conversation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the magicians disputed the matter among themselves, conversing privately.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَٰنِ لَسَٰحِرَٰنِ يُرِيدَانِ أَن يُخۡرِجَاكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُم بِسِحۡرِهِمَا وَيَذۡهَبَا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ ٱلۡمُثۡلَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo in hathani lasahirani yureedani an yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihima wayathhaba bitareeqatikumu almuthla
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Indeed, these are two magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic and do away with your most exemplary way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They concluded, “These two are only magicians who want to drive you out of your land with their magic, and do away with your most cherished traditions.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَجۡمِعُواْ كَيۡدَكُمۡ ثُمَّ ٱئۡتُواْ صَفّٗاۚ وَقَدۡ أَفۡلَحَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ مَنِ ٱسۡتَعۡلَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FaajmiAAoo kaydakum thumma itoo saffan waqad aflaha alyawma mani istaAAla
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So resolve upon your plan and then come [forward] in line. And he has succeeded today who overcomes."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So orchestrate your plan, then come forward in ˹perfect˺ ranks. And whoever prevails today will certainly be successful.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلۡقِيَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنۡ أَلۡقَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ya moosa imma an tulqiya waimma an nakoona awwala man alqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "O Moses, either you throw or we will be the first to throw."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They said, “O Moses! Either you cast, or let us be the first to cast.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ بَلۡ أَلۡقُواْۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمۡ وَعِصِيُّهُمۡ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيۡهِ مِن سِحۡرِهِمۡ أَنَّهَا تَسۡعَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala bal alqoo faitha hibaluhum waAAisiyyuhum yukhayyalu ilayhi min sihrihim annaha tasAAa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Rather, you throw." And suddenly their ropes and staffs seemed to him from their magic that they were moving [like snakes].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses responded, “No, you go first.” And suddenly their ropes and staffs appeared to him—by their magic—to be slithering.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَوۡجَسَ فِي نَفۡسِهِۦ خِيفَةٗ مُّوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faawjasa fee nafsihi kheefatan moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he sensed within himself apprehension, did Moses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So Moses concealed fear within himself.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡنَا لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qulna la takhaf innaka anta alaAAla
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah said, "Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We reassured ˹him˺, “Do not fear! It is certainly you who will prevail.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَلۡقِ مَا فِي يَمِينِكَ تَلۡقَفۡ مَا صَنَعُوٓاْۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُواْ كَيۡدُ سَٰحِرٖۖ وَلَا يُفۡلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَيۡثُ أَتَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalqi ma fee yameenika talqaf ma sanaAAoo innama sanaAAoo kaydu sahirin wala yuflihu alssahiru haythu ata
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted. What they have crafted is but the trick of a magician, and the magician will not succeed wherever he is."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Cast what is in your right hand, and it will swallow up what they have made, for what they have made is no more than a magic trick. And magicians can never succeed wherever they go.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأُلۡقِيَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدٗا قَالُوٓاْ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faolqiya alssaharatu sujjadan qaloo amanna birabbi haroona wamoosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, "We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the magicians fell down in prostration, declaring, “We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحۡرَۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيۡدِيَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَٰفٖ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ فِي جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخۡلِ وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ أَيُّنَآ أَشَدُّ عَذَابٗا وَأَبۡقَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athana lakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu alssihra falaoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakum min khilafin walaosallibannakum fee juthooAAi alnnakhli walataAAlamunna ayyuna ashaddu AAathaban waabqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed him before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic. So I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will surely know which of us is more severe in [giving] punishment and more enduring."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Pharaoh threatened, “How dare you believe in him before I give you permission? He must be your master who taught you magic. I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. You will really see whose punishment is more severe and more lasting.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَن نُّؤۡثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ وَٱلَّذِي فَطَرَنَاۖ فَٱقۡضِ مَآ أَنتَ قَاضٍۖ إِنَّمَا تَقۡضِي هَٰذِهِ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنۡيَآ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo lan nuthiraka AAala ma jaana mina albayyinati waallathee fatarana faiqdi ma anta qadin innama taqdee hathihi alhayata alddunya
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Never will we prefer you over what has come to us of clear proofs and [over] He who created us. So decree whatever you are to decree. You can only decree for this worldly life.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They responded, “By the One Who created us! We will never prefer you over the clear proofs that have come to us. So do whatever you want! Your authority only covers the ˹fleeting˺ life of this world.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّنَا لِيَغۡفِرَ لَنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَا وَمَآ أَكۡرَهۡتَنَا عَلَيۡهِ مِنَ ٱلسِّحۡرِۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَيۡرٞ وَأَبۡقَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna amanna birabbina liyaghfira lana khatayana wama akrahtana AAalayhi mina alssihri waAllahu khayrun waabqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our sins and what you compelled us [to do] of magic. And Allah is better and more enduring."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, we have believed in our Lord so He may forgive our sins and that magic you have forced us to practice. And Allah is far superior ˹in reward˺ and more lasting ˹in punishment˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَأۡتِ رَبَّهُۥ مُجۡرِمٗا فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحۡيَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innahu man yati rabbahu mujriman fainna lahu jahannama la yamootu feeha wala yahya
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal - indeed, for him is Hell; he will neither die therein nor live.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whoever comes to their Lord as an evildoer will certainly have Hell, where they can neither live nor die.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن يَأۡتِهِۦ مُؤۡمِنٗا قَدۡ عَمِلَ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلدَّرَجَٰتُ ٱلۡعُلَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman yatihi muminan qad AAamila alssalihati faolaika lahumu alddarajatu alAAula
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But whoever comes to Him as a believer having done righteous deeds - for those will be the highest degrees [in position]:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But whoever comes to Him as a believer, having done good, they will have the highest ranks:

ayahs.textArabic: جَنَّٰتُ عَدۡنٖ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِهَا ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَاۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Jannatu AAadnin tajree min tahtiha alanharu khalideena feeha wathalika jazao man tazakka
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. And that is the reward of one who purifies himself.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Gardens of Eternity, under which rivers flow, where they will stay forever. That is the reward of those who purify themselves.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنۡ أَسۡرِ بِعِبَادِي فَٱضۡرِبۡ لَهُمۡ طَرِيقٗا فِي ٱلۡبَحۡرِ يَبَسٗا لَّا تَخَٰفُ دَرَكٗا وَلَا تَخۡشَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad awhayna ila moosa an asri biAAibadee faidrib lahum tareeqan fee albahri yabasan la takhafu darakan wala takhsha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken [by Pharaoh] nor be afraid [of drowning]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We surely inspired Moses, ˹saying,˺ “Leave with My servants ˹at night˺ and strike a dry passage for them across the sea. Have no fear of being overtaken, nor be concerned ˹of drowning˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَتۡبَعَهُمۡ فِرۡعَوۡنُ بِجُنُودِهِۦ فَغَشِيَهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡيَمِّ مَا غَشِيَهُمۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FaatbaAAahum firAAawnu bijunoodihi faghashiyahum mina alyammi ma ghashiyahum
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers, and there covered them from the sea that which covered them,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then Pharaoh pursued them with his soldiers—but how overwhelming were the waters that submerged them!

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَضَلَّ فِرۡعَوۡنُ قَوۡمَهُۥ وَمَا هَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waadalla firAAawnu qawmahu wama hada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide [them].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹so˺ Pharaoh led his people astray, and did not guide ˹them rightly˺.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰبَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ قَدۡ أَنجَيۡنَٰكُم مِّنۡ عَدُوِّكُمۡ وَوَٰعَدۡنَٰكُمۡ جَانِبَ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلۡأَيۡمَنَ وَنَزَّلۡنَا عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلۡوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya banee israeela qad anjaynakum min AAaduwwikum wawaAAadnakum janiba alttoori alaymana wanazzalna AAalaykumu almanna waalssalwa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O Children of Israel, We delivered you from your enemy, and We made an appointment with you at the right side of the mount, and We sent down to you manna and quails,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O Children of Israel! We saved you from your enemy, and made an appointment with you on the right side of Mount Ṭûr, and sent down to you manna and quails,

ayahs.textArabic: كُلُواْ مِن طَيِّبَٰتِ مَا رَزَقۡنَٰكُمۡ وَلَا تَطۡغَوۡاْ فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيۡكُمۡ غَضَبِيۖ وَمَن يَحۡلِلۡ عَلَيۡهِ غَضَبِي فَقَدۡ هَوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kuloo min tayyibati ma razaqnakum wala tatghaw feehi fayahilla AAalaykum ghadabee waman yahlil AAalayhi ghadabee faqad hawa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you and do not transgress [or oppress others] therein, lest My anger should descend upon you. And he upon whom My anger descends has certainly fallen."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹saying,˺ “Eat from the good things We have provided for you, but do not transgress in them, or My wrath will befall you. And whoever My wrath befalls is certainly doomed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٞ لِّمَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحٗا ثُمَّ ٱهۡتَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnee laghaffarun liman taba waamana waAAamila salihan thumma ihtada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But indeed, I am the Perpetual Forgiver of whoever repents and believes and does righteousness and then continues in guidance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But I am truly Most Forgiving to whoever repents, believes, and does good, then persists on ˹true˺ guidance.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَمَآ أَعۡجَلَكَ عَن قَوۡمِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama aAAjalaka AAan qawmika ya moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "And what made you hasten from your people, O Moses?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah asked,˺ “Why have you come with such haste ahead of your people, O Moses?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ هُمۡ أُوْلَآءِ عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِي وَعَجِلۡتُ إِلَيۡكَ رَبِّ لِتَرۡضَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala hum olai AAala atharee waAAajiltu ilayka rabbi litarda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You be pleased."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “They are close on my tracks. And I have hastened to You, my Lord, so You will be pleased.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّا قَدۡ فَتَنَّا قَوۡمَكَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ ٱلسَّامِرِيُّ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fainna qad fatanna qawmaka min baAAdika waadallahumu alssamiriyyu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "But indeed, We have tried your people after you [departed], and the Samiri has led them astray."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah responded, “We have indeed tested your people in your absence, and the Sâmiri has led them astray.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَرَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمِهِۦ غَضۡبَٰنَ أَسِفٗاۚ قَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ أَلَمۡ يَعِدۡكُمۡ رَبُّكُمۡ وَعۡدًا حَسَنًاۚ أَفَطَالَ عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡعَهۡدُ أَمۡ أَرَدتُّمۡ أَن يَحِلَّ عَلَيۡكُمۡ غَضَبٞ مِّن رَّبِّكُمۡ فَأَخۡلَفۡتُم مَّوۡعِدِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: FarajaAAa moosa ila qawmihi ghadbana asifan qala ya qawmi alam yaAAidkum rabbukum waAAdan hasanan afatala AAalaykumu alAAahdu am aradtum an yahilla AAalaykum ghadabun min rabbikum faakhlaftum mawAAidee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said, "O my people, did your Lord not make you a good promise? Then, was the time [of its fulfillment] too long for you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord descend upon you, so you broke your promise [of obedience] to me?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So Moses returned to his people, furious and sorrowful. He said, “O my people! Had your Lord not made you a good promise? Has my absence been too long for you? Or have you wished for wrath from your Lord to befall you, so you broke your promise to me?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ مَآ أَخۡلَفۡنَا مَوۡعِدَكَ بِمَلۡكِنَا وَلَٰكِنَّا حُمِّلۡنَآ أَوۡزَارٗا مِّن زِينَةِ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ فَقَذَفۡنَٰهَا فَكَذَٰلِكَ أَلۡقَى ٱلسَّامِرِيُّ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ma akhlafna mawAAidaka bimalkina walakinna hummilna awzaran min zeenati alqawmi faqathafnaha fakathalika alqa alssamiriyyu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We did not break our promise to you by our will, but we were made to carry burdens from the ornaments of the people [of Pharaoh], so we threw them [into the fire], and thus did the Samiri throw."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They argued, “We did not break our promise to you of our own free will, but we were made to carry the burden of the people’s ˹golden˺ jewellery, then we threw it ˹into the fire˺, and so did the Sâmiri.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَخۡرَجَ لَهُمۡ عِجۡلٗا جَسَدٗا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٞ فَقَالُواْ هَٰذَآ إِلَٰهُكُمۡ وَإِلَٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِيَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakhraja lahum AAijlan jasadan lahu khuwarun faqaloo hatha ilahukum wailahu moosa fanasiya
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he moulded for them an idol of a calf that made a lowing sound. They said, “This is your god and the god of Moses, but Moses forgot ˹where it was˺!”

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَلَا يَرَوۡنَ أَلَّا يَرۡجِعُ إِلَيۡهِمۡ قَوۡلٗا وَلَا يَمۡلِكُ لَهُمۡ ضَرّٗا وَلَا نَفۡعٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afala yarawna alla yarjiAAu ilayhim qawlan wala yamliku lahum darran wala nafAAan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Did they not see that it could not return to them any speech and that it did not possess for them any harm or benefit?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Did they not see that it did not respond to them, nor could it protect or benefit them?

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ هَٰرُونُ مِن قَبۡلُ يَٰقَوۡمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِي وَأَطِيعُوٓاْ أَمۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad qala lahum haroonu min qablu ya qawmi innama futintum bihi wainna rabbakumu alrrahmanu faittabiAAoonee waateeAAoo amree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Aaron had already told them before [the return of Moses], "O my people, you are only being tested by it, and indeed, your Lord is the Most Merciful, so follow me and obey my order."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Aaron had already warned them beforehand, “O my people! You are only being tested by this, for indeed your ˹one true˺ Lord is the Most Compassionate. So follow me and obey my orders.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ لَن نَّبۡرَحَ عَلَيۡهِ عَٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرۡجِعَ إِلَيۡنَا مُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo lan nabraha AAalayhi AAakifeena hatta yarjiAAa ilayna moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We will never cease being devoted to the calf until Moses returns to us."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “We will not cease to worship it until Moses returns to us.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ يَٰهَٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذۡ رَأَيۡتَهُمۡ ضَلُّوٓاْ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ya haroonu ma manaAAaka ith raaytahum dalloo
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "O Aaron, what prevented you, when you saw them going astray,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses scolded ˹his brother˺, “O Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِۖ أَفَعَصَيۡتَ أَمۡرِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alla tattabiAAani afaAAasayta amree
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From following me? Then have you disobeyed my order?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from following after me? How could you disobey my orders?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ يَبۡنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأۡخُذۡ بِلِحۡيَتِي وَلَا بِرَأۡسِيٓۖ إِنِّي خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقۡتَ بَيۡنَ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمۡ تَرۡقُبۡ قَوۡلِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ya bna omma la takhuth bilihyatee wala birasee innee khasheetu an taqoola farraqta bayna banee israeela walam tarqub qawlee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Aaron] said, "O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard or by my head. Indeed, I feared that you would say, 'You caused division among the Children of Israel, and you did not observe [or await] my word.' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Aaron pleaded, “O son of my mother! Do not seize me by my beard or ˹the hair of˺ my head. I really feared that you would say, ‘You have caused division among the Children of Israel, and did not observe my word.’”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكَ يَٰسَٰمِرِيُّ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fama khatbuka ya samiriyyu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "And what is your case, O Samiri?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses then asked, “What did you think you were doing, O Sâmiri?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ بَصُرۡتُ بِمَا لَمۡ يَبۡصُرُواْ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضۡتُ قَبۡضَةٗ مِّنۡ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذۡتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتۡ لِي نَفۡسِي
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala basurtu bima lam yabsuroo bihi faqabadtu qabdatan min athari alrrasooli fanabathtuha wakathalika sawwalat lee nafsee
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful [of dust] from the track of the messenger and threw it, and thus did my soul entice me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He said, “I saw what they did not see, so I took a handful ˹of dust˺ from the hoof-prints of ˹the horse of˺ the messenger-angel ˹Gabriel˺ then cast it ˹on the moulded calf˺. This is what my lower-self tempted me into.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَٱذۡهَبۡ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّن تُخۡلَفَهُۥۖ وَٱنظُرۡ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِي ظَلۡتَ عَلَيۡهِ عَاكِفٗاۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ نَسۡفًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala faithhab fainna laka fee alhayati an taqoola la misasa wainna laka mawAAidan lan tukhlafahu waonthur ila ilahika allathee thalta AAalayhi AAakifan lanuharriqannahu thumma lanansifannahu fee alyammi nasfan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it into the sea with a blast.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moses said, “Go away then! And for ˹the rest of your˺ life you will surely be crying, ‘Do not touch ˹me˺!’ Then you will certainly have a fate that you cannot escape. Now look at your god to which you have been devoted: we will burn it up, then scatter it in the sea completely.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِي لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ عِلۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innama ilahukumu Allahu allathee la ilaha illa huwa wasiAAa kulla shayin AAilman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Your god is only Allah, except for whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then Moses addressed his people,˺ “Your only god is Allah, there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. He encompasses everything in ˹His˺ knowledge.”

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدۡ سَبَقَۚ وَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kathalika naqussu AAalayka min anbai ma qad sabaqa waqad ataynaka min ladunna thikran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thus, [O Muhammad], We relate to you from the news of what has preceded. And We have certainly given you from Us the Qur'an.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is how We relate to you ˹O Prophet˺ some of the stories of the past. And We have certainly granted you a Reminder from Us.

ayahs.textArabic: مَّنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَنۡهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَحۡمِلُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وِزۡرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Man aAArada AAanhu fainnahu yahmilu yawma alqiyamati wizran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Whoever turns away from it - then indeed, he will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whoever turns away from it will surely bear the burden ˹of sin˺ on the Day of Judgment,

ayahs.textArabic: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهِۖ وَسَآءَ لَهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ حِمۡلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Khalideena feehi wasaa lahum yawma alqiyamati himlan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Abiding] eternally therein, and evil it is for them on the Day of Resurrection as a load -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: suffering its consequences forever. What an evil burden they will carry on Judgment Day!

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يُنفَخُ فِي ٱلصُّورِۚ وَنَحۡشُرُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ يَوۡمَئِذٖ زُرۡقٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yunfakhu fee alssoori wanahshuru almujrimeena yawmaithin zurqan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day the Horn will be blown. And We will gather the criminals, that Day, blue-eyed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the Trumpet will be blown, and We will gather the wicked on that Day blue-faced ˹from horror and thirst˺.

ayahs.textArabic: يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ بَيۡنَهُمۡ إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا عَشۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yatakhafatoona baynahum in labithtum illa AAashran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will murmur among themselves, "You remained not but ten [days in the world]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will whisper among themselves, “You stayed no more than ten days ˹on the earth˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: نَّحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذۡ يَقُولُ أَمۡثَلُهُمۡ طَرِيقَةً إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا يَوۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yaqooloona ith yaqoolu amthaluhum tareeqatan in labithtum illa yawman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We are most knowing of what they say when the best of them in manner will say, "You remained not but one day."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We know best what they will say—the most reasonable of them will say, “You stayed no more than a day.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَسۡـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلۡجِبَالِ فَقُلۡ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّي نَسۡفٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayasaloonaka AAani aljibali faqul yansifuha rabbee nasfan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they ask you about the mountains, so say, "My Lord will blow them away with a blast.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹if˺ they ask you ˹O Prophet˺ about the mountains, ˹then˺ say, “My Lord will wipe them out completely,

ayahs.textArabic: فَيَذَرُهَا قَاعٗا صَفۡصَفٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fayatharuha qaAAan safsafan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And He will leave the earth a level plain;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: leaving the earth level and bare,

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجٗا وَلَآ أَمۡتٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: La tara feeha AAiwajan wala amtan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You will not see therein a depression or an elevation."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with neither depressions nor elevations to be seen.”

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِيَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلۡأَصۡوَاتُ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ فَلَا تَسۡمَعُ إِلَّا هَمۡسٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawmaithin yattabiAAoona alddaAAiya la AAiwaja lahu wakhashaAAati alaswatu lilrrahmani fala tasmaAAu illa hamsan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That Day, everyone will follow [the call of] the Caller [with] no deviation therefrom, and [all] voices will be stilled before the Most Merciful, so you will not hear except a whisper [of footsteps].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day all will follow the caller ˹for assembly˺, ˹and˺ none will dare to deviate. All voices will be hushed before the Most Compassionate. Only whispers will be heard.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ لَّا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةُ إِلَّا مَنۡ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَرَضِيَ لَهُۥ قَوۡلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawmaithin la tanfaAAu alshshafaAAatu illa man athina lahu alrrahmanu waradiya lahu qawlan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That Day, no intercession will benefit except [that of] one to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day no intercession will be of any benefit, except by those granted permission by the Most Compassionate and whose words are agreeable to Him.

ayahs.textArabic: يَعۡلَمُ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِيهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِهِۦ عِلۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yuheetoona bihi AAilman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, but they do not encompass it in knowledge.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He ˹fully˺ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, but they cannot encompass Him in ˹their˺ knowledge.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَعَنَتِ ٱلۡوُجُوهُ لِلۡحَيِّ ٱلۡقَيُّومِۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنۡ حَمَلَ ظُلۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAanati alwujoohu lilhayyi alqayyoomi waqad khaba man hamala thulman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [all] faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. And he will have failed who carries injustice.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And all faces will be humbled before the Ever-Living, All-Sustaining. And those burdened with wrongdoing will be in loss.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤۡمِنٞ فَلَا يَخَافُ ظُلۡمٗا وَلَا هَضۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman yaAAmal mina alssalihati wahuwa muminun fala yakhafu thulman wala hadman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But he who does of righteous deeds while he is a believer - he will neither fear injustice nor deprivation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But whoever does good and is a believer will have no fear of being wronged or denied ˹their reward˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُ قُرۡءَانًا عَرَبِيّٗا وَصَرَّفۡنَا فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلۡوَعِيدِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَتَّقُونَ أَوۡ يُحۡدِثُ لَهُمۡ ذِكۡرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakathalika anzalnahu quranan AAarabiyyan wasarrafna feehi mina alwaAAeedi laAAallahum yattaqoona aw yuhdithu lahum thikran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur'an and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid [sin] or it would cause them remembrance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And so We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran and varied the warnings in it, so perhaps they will shun evil or it may cause them to be mindful.

ayahs.textArabic: فَتَعَٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡمَلِكُ ٱلۡحَقُّۗ وَلَا تَعۡجَلۡ بِٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مِن قَبۡلِ أَن يُقۡضَىٰٓ إِلَيۡكَ وَحۡيُهُۥۖ وَقُل رَّبِّ زِدۡنِي عِلۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: FataAAala Allahu almaliku alhaqqu wala taAAjal bialqurani min qabli an yuqda ilayka wahyuhu waqul rabbi zidnee AAilman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So high [above all] is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth. And, [O Muhammad], do not hasten with [recitation of] the Qur'an before its revelation is completed to you, and say, "My Lord, increase me in knowledge."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Exalted is Allah, the True King! Do not rush to recite ˹a revelation of˺ the Quran ˹O Prophet˺ before it is ˹properly˺ conveyed to you, and pray, “My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ عَهِدۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ مِن قَبۡلُ فَنَسِيَ وَلَمۡ نَجِدۡ لَهُۥ عَزۡمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad AAahidna ila adama min qablu fanasiya walam najid lahu AAazman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had already taken a promise from Adam before, but he forgot; and We found not in him determination.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We once made a covenant with Adam, but he forgot, and ˹so˺ We did not find determination in him.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذۡ قُلۡنَا لِلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسۡجُدُواْ لِأٓدَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓاْ إِلَّآ إِبۡلِيسَ أَبَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waith qulna lilmalaikati osjudoo liadama fasajadoo illa ibleesa aba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] when We said to the angels, "Prostrate to Adam," and they prostrated, except Iblees; he refused.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when We said to the angels, “Prostrate before Adam,” so they all did—but not Iblîs, who refused ˹arrogantly˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقُلۡنَا يَٰٓـَٔادَمُ إِنَّ هَٰذَا عَدُوّٞ لَّكَ وَلِزَوۡجِكَ فَلَا يُخۡرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ فَتَشۡقَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqulna ya adamu inna hatha AAaduwwun laka walizawjika fala yukhrijannakuma mina aljannati fatashqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We said, "O Adam, indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Then let him not remove you from Paradise so you would suffer.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We cautioned, “O Adam! This is surely an enemy to you and to your wife. So do not let him drive you both out of Paradise, for you ˹O Adam˺ would then suffer ˹hardship˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيهَا وَلَا تَعۡرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna laka alla tajooAAa feeha wala taAAra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is [promised] for you not to be hungry therein or be unclothed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Here it is guaranteed that you will never go hungry or unclothed,

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَنَّكَ لَا تَظۡمَؤُاْ فِيهَا وَلَا تَضۡحَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waannaka la tathmao feeha wala tadha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, you will not be thirsty therein or be hot from the sun."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor will you ˹ever˺ suffer from thirst or ˹the sun’s˺ heat.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَوَسۡوَسَ إِلَيۡهِ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ قَالَ يَٰٓـَٔادَمُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ ٱلۡخُلۡدِ وَمُلۡكٖ لَّا يَبۡلَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fawaswasa ilayhi alshshaytanu qala ya adamu hal adulluka AAala shajarati alkhuldi wamulkin la yabla
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then Satan whispered to him; he said, "O Adam, shall I direct you to the tree of eternity and possession that will not deteriorate?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But Satan whispered to him, saying, “O Adam! Shall I show you the Tree of Immortality and a kingdom that does not fade away?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَكَلَا مِنۡهَا فَبَدَتۡ لَهُمَا سَوۡءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخۡصِفَانِ عَلَيۡهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلۡجَنَّةِۚ وَعَصَىٰٓ ءَادَمُ رَبَّهُۥ فَغَوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faakala minha fabadat lahuma sawatuhuma watafiqa yakhsifani AAalayhima min waraqi aljannati waAAasa adamu rabbahu faghawa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Adam and his wife ate of it, and their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten over themselves from the leaves of Paradise. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they both ate from the tree and then their nakedness was exposed to them, prompting them to cover themselves with leaves from Paradise. So Adam disobeyed his Lord, and ˹so˺ lost his way.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ ٱجۡتَبَٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَتَابَ عَلَيۡهِ وَهَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma ijtabahu rabbuhu fataba AAalayhi wahada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then his Lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided [him].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then his Lord chose him ˹for His grace˺, accepted his repentance, and guided him ˹rightly˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ ٱهۡبِطَا مِنۡهَا جَمِيعَۢاۖ بَعۡضُكُمۡ لِبَعۡضٍ عَدُوّٞۖ فَإِمَّا يَأۡتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّي هُدٗى فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشۡقَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ihbita minha jameeAAan baAAdukum libaAAdin AAaduwwun faimma yatiyannakum minnee hudan famani ittabaAAa hudaya fala yadillu wala yashqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "Descend from Paradise - all, [your descendants] being enemies to one another. And if there should come to you guidance from Me - then whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray [in the world] nor suffer [in the Hereafter].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah said, “Descend, both of you, from here together ˹with Satan˺ as enemies to each other. Then when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray ˹in this life˺ nor suffer ˹in the next˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَن ذِكۡرِي فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةٗ ضَنكٗا وَنَحۡشُرُهُۥ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ أَعۡمَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman aAArada AAan thikree fainna lahu maAAeeshatan dankan wanahshuruhu yawma alqiyamati aAAman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed life, and We will gather him on the Day of Resurrection blind."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But whoever turns away from My Reminder will certainly have a miserable life, then We will raise them up blind on the Day of Judgment.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرۡتَنِيٓ أَعۡمَىٰ وَقَدۡ كُنتُ بَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi lima hashartanee aAAma waqad kuntu baseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will cry, “My Lord! Why have you raised me up blind, although I used to see?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ أَتَتۡكَ ءَايَٰتُنَا فَنَسِيتَهَاۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ تُنسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala kathalika atatka ayatuna fanaseetaha wakathalika alyawma tunsa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] will say, "Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah will respond, “It is so, just as Our revelations came to you and you neglected them, so Today you are neglected.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي مَنۡ أَسۡرَفَ وَلَمۡ يُؤۡمِنۢ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِۦۚ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبۡقَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakathalika najzee man asrafa walam yumin biayati rabbihi walaAAathabu alakhirati ashaddu waabqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And thus do We recompense he who transgressed and did not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is more severe and more enduring.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is how We reward whoever transgresses and does not believe in the revelations of their Lord. And the punishment of the Hereafter is far more severe and more lasting.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَلَمۡ يَهۡدِ لَهُمۡ كَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا قَبۡلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡقُرُونِ يَمۡشُونَ فِي مَسَٰكِنِهِمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّأُوْلِي ٱلنُّهَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afalam yahdi lahum kam ahlakna qablahum mina alqurooni yamshoona fee masakinihim inna fee thalika laayatin liolee alnnuha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then, has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them as they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs for those of intelligence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it not yet clear to them how many peoples We destroyed before them, whose ruins they still pass by? Surely in this are signs for people of sound judgment.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡلَا كَلِمَةٞ سَبَقَتۡ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَكَانَ لِزَامٗا وَأَجَلٞ مُّسَمّٗى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walawla kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika lakana lizaman waajalun musamman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, punishment would have been an obligation [due immediately], and [if not for] a specified term [decreed].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord ˹O Prophet˺ and a term already set, their ˹instant˺ doom would have been inevitable.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّكَ قَبۡلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمۡسِ وَقَبۡلَ غُرُوبِهَاۖ وَمِنۡ ءَانَآيِٕ ٱلَّيۡلِ فَسَبِّحۡ وَأَطۡرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرۡضَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faisbir AAala ma yaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAi alshshamsi waqabla ghuroobiha wamin anai allayli fasabbih waatrafa alnnahari laAAallaka tarda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So be patient over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during periods of the night [exalt Him] and at the ends of the day, that you may be satisfied.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say. And glorify the praises of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset, and glorify Him in the hours of the night and at both ends of the day, so that you may be pleased ˹with the reward˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيۡنَيۡكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّنۡهُمۡ زَهۡرَةَ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَا لِنَفۡتِنَهُمۡ فِيهِۚ وَرِزۡقُ رَبِّكَ خَيۡرٞ وَأَبۡقَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum zahrata alhayati alddunya linaftinahum feehi warizqu rabbika khayrun waabqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [some] categories of them, [its being but] the splendor of worldly life by which We test them. And the provision of your Lord is better and more enduring.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do not let your eyes crave what We have allowed some of the disbelievers to enjoy; the ˹fleeting˺ splendour of this worldly life, which We test them with. But your Lord’s provision ˹in the Hereafter˺ is far better and more lasting.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأۡمُرۡ أَهۡلَكَ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ عَلَيۡهَاۖ لَا نَسۡـَٔلُكَ رِزۡقٗاۖ نَّحۡنُ نَرۡزُقُكَۗ وَٱلۡعَٰقِبَةُ لِلتَّقۡوَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wamur ahlaka bialssalati waistabir AAalayha la nasaluka rizqan nahnu narzuquka waalAAaqibatu lilttaqwa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And enjoin prayer upon your family [and people] and be steadfast therein. We ask you not for provision; We provide for you, and the [best] outcome is for [those of] righteousness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Bid your people to pray, and be diligent in ˹observing˺ it. We do not ask you to provide. It is We Who provide for you. And the ultimate outcome is ˹only˺ for ˹the people of˺ righteousness.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ لَوۡلَا يَأۡتِينَا بِـَٔايَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓۚ أَوَلَمۡ تَأۡتِهِم بَيِّنَةُ مَا فِي ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo lawla yateena biayatin min rabbihi awalam tatihim bayyinatu ma fee alssuhufi aloola
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?" Has there not come to them evidence of what was in the former scriptures?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They demand, “If only he could bring us a sign from his Lord!” Have they not ˹already˺ received a confirmation of what is in earlier Scriptures?

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡ أَنَّآ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهُم بِعَذَابٖ مِّن قَبۡلِهِۦ لَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَا لَوۡلَآ أَرۡسَلۡتَ إِلَيۡنَا رَسُولٗا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَٰتِكَ مِن قَبۡلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخۡزَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaw anna ahlaknahum biAAathabin min qablihi laqaloo rabbana lawla arsalta ilayna rasoolan fanattabiAAa ayatika min qabli an nathilla wanakhza
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if We had destroyed them with a punishment before him, they would have said, "Our Lord, why did You not send to us a messenger so we could have followed Your verses before we were humiliated and disgraced?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had We destroyed them with a torment before this ˹Prophet came˺, they would have surely argued, “Our Lord! If only You had sent us a messenger, we would have followed Your revelations before being humiliated and put to shame.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ كُلّٞ مُّتَرَبِّصٞ فَتَرَبَّصُواْۖ فَسَتَعۡلَمُونَ مَنۡ أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ ٱلسَّوِيِّ وَمَنِ ٱهۡتَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul kullun mutarabbisun fatarabbasoo fasataAAlamoona man ashabu alssirati alssawiyyi wamani ihtada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Each [of us] is waiting; so wait. For you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say ˹to them, O Prophet˺, “Each ˹of us˺ is waiting, so keep waiting! You will soon know who is on the Straight Path and is ˹rightly˺ guided.”
